macro_segments.title: Introduction to the Fourth Perek and Overview of the Mishna on Eleven Days

micro_segments.title: Context of Fourth Perek and transition from Yoledet discussion
The Mishna: All eleven days are in a state of purity (bechezkat tahara)
Explanation of the eleven days and the logic of menstrual cycles

macro_segments.title: Checking Requirements: General obligations, failure to check, and the day of expected period

micro_segments.title: Woman who did not check when supposed to (Yashva lo velo badka)
Status when the time of expected period arrives without checking
Rabbi Meir's exception: hiding from oppressors suspends bleeding (fear overrides)
Zav, Zavah, and Shomeret Yom Kineged Yom status and checking during clean days

macro_segments.title: Gemara's Investigation: Purpose of the eleven-day presumption of purity (Lemai Hilchata)

micro_segments.title: Rav Yehuda's answer: She does not need to check during these days
Gemara's question: How does this fit with the rule that all women must check twice daily?
Resolution: Seifa refers to different Halacha; reading the Mishna in two parts

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Meir's Opinion and Application to women without fixed period (Ashan she-ein lah veset)

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Meir: Woman without fixed period must be divorced (Yotzi ve-lo Yachzir)
Concern about transgression: Fear of illicit relations during non-safe days
Gemara's reading: Seifa is within Rabbi Meir's opinion with qualifications

macro_segments.title: Proposed Answers for the Purpose of the Eleven-Day Presumption

micro_segments.title: Third answer (Rava): Does not need to be concerned 24 hours retroactively (metame'ah me-et le-et)
Braita contradiction: Woman who is Zavah must be concerned retroactively
Fourth answer (Rav Huna bar Chiya citing Shmuel): Cannot establish fixed period during Zivah days

macro_segments.title: The Principle of Veset Kavua (Fixed Period) and its Halachic Implications

micro_segments.title: Definition and relevance of fixed period (Veset Kavua)
Stringencies of Veset Kavua: Concern persists until reversed three times

macro_segments.title: Rav Yosef's Challenge and Clarification: Counting from Mikva vs. from bleeding

micro_segments.title: Rav Yosef does not remember hearing this ruling (lo shmi'a li)

macro_segments.title: The Principle of Veset Kavua (Fixed Period) and its Halachic Implications (continued)

micro_segments.title: Mishna example: Woman switching from day 15 to day 20 (both forbidden until pattern shifts)

macro_segments.title: Rav Yosef's Challenge and Clarification: Counting from Mikva vs. from bleeding (continued)

micro_segments.title: Abaye's response: You taught us this based on the Mishna
Clarification: Day 15 in the Mishna means day 15 from Mikva, not from onset of bleeding

macro_segments.title: Rav Papa's Inquiry and the Hen Analogy: Does Zivah period establish any concern?

micro_segments.title: Rav Papa's question to Rav Yehuda: Is Zivah period a matter of concern even if not fixed?
Rav Huna bar Yehoshua's objection: Count from actual bleeding, not virtual
The hen laying eggs analogy: Pattern resets from actual occurrence, not expected

macro_segments.title: Debate between Reish Lakish and Rabbi Yochanan: Can a Veset establish during Zivah period?

micro_segments.title: Reish Lakish: Woman establishes Veset during Zivah period but not during Niddah period
Rabbi Yochanan: Woman can establish Veset even during Niddah period
Example of Rabbi Yochanan: Bleeding on Rosh Chodesh and day 5 establishing pattern
Objection and clarification: Case involves late start of period, not virtual counting
Final resolution by Ravin: Count from actual bleeding, confirming earlier principle

macro_segments.title: Summary: Three unresolved possibilities for cycles less than 19 days

micro_segments.title: Possibility 1: Cycle can be a fixed Veset Kavua
Possibility 2: Not fixed Veset but must be concerned (Chosheish)
Possibility 3: No concern required; irrelevant for practice

topical_tags.term: Bechezkat Tahara (presumption of purity)
Yoledet (woman after childbirth)
Zavah / Zivah (woman with bleeding during non-menstrual days)
Veset (menstrual pattern/period)
Veset Kavua (fixed menstrual period)
Checking / Bedika
Metame'ah me-et le-et (retroactive concern 24 hours back)
Rabbi Meir's ruling on woman without fixed period
Shomeret Yom Kineged Yom (Zavah Ketana - one clean day requirement)
Niddah (menstruating woman)
Rav Yehuda (Amora; student of Shmuel)
Shmuel (Amora; source of ruling on Veset)
Rav Huna bar Chiya (Amora; transmits Shmuel's view)
Rav Yosef (student of Rav Yehuda)
Abaye (student of Rav Yosef)
Rav Papa (Amora; poses inquiry about Zivah concerns)
Rav Huna bar Yehoshua (challenges Rav Papa's reasoning)
Reish Lakish (Amora; holds Veset can establish during Zivah)
Rabbi Yochanan (Amora; disputes Reish Lakish)
Ravin (Amora; confirms counting from actual, not virtual)
Higia Zman Vesta (time of expected period arrives)
Fear suspends bleeding (Charada mesalekes et hadamim)
Chezka / Chezkat Tumah (presumption of impurity)
Shiva Nekim (seven clean days)
Mesulakot Damim (women whose menstrual flow is suspended)
Counting from Mikva vs. from bleeding onset
Hen analogy for establishing pattern
Tosefet (additional bleeding at start of period)
